textPlain: The problem with trying to just throw money at a problem to hire a bunch of “top” talent and “leading visionaries” is that those egos rarely play well together under the same roof.Culture, or the lack thereof, eats your unlimited budget for lunch. There are indeed problems that money can’t solve. That’s playing out painfully at Meta right now.

> Meta said this allegation was “manufactured tension without basis in fact that’s clearly being pushed by dramatic, navel-gazing busybodies.”Was this bombastic, “nyah-nyah,” “I know you are but what am I” style of public communication a thing before the current president hired spokespeople who behaved this way?If it’s at all indicative of how things work inside that company, I’d be out the door on the first day too, no matter how many absurd millions were supposedly on offer…
> “While TBD Labs is still relatively new, we believe it has the greatest compute-per-researcher in the industry, and that will only increase,” Meta said.Well, two ways to make that true!
Maybe Zuck’s “Just keep throwing money at it” strategy ain’t all that.
Teething pains are surely expected. But the egos of some of these people have to be inflated worse than ai wrapper valuations.
Wow what could possibly be so bad that multiple people leave after just some days

When the first names were announced I read some comments on r/LocalLLaMA that they assembled a team with too many high egos and this was going to fall apart, because of general soft-skills issues.I know nothing about the situation, but that comment was strangely specific
